title: The Winners and Losers of the 2026 Oscars
text: At the 98th Academy Awards, hosted by Conan O'Brien, several notable outcomes emerged. Major Winners: "One Battle After Another" (Warner Bros.) claimed Best Picture and six total Oscars, with Paul Thomas Anderson winning for both directing and adapted screenplay. "Sinners" earned four Oscars, including Best Original Screenplay for Ryan Coogler and Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan. Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history as the first woman to win Best Cinematography. "KPop Demon Hunters" won Best Animated Feature, with its song "Golden" becoming "the first K-pop song to be honored by the Oscars." Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress for "Weapons," earning her first Oscar after 40 years. Notable Losers: Timothée Chalamet lost the Best Actor race despite his Golden Globe win for "Marty Supreme." The ceremony ran nearly 3 hours 45 minutes, with speech cutoffs angering K-pop fans. Trivia: The Oscars recorded their seventh tie in history when two films won Best Live Action Short Film.
